title: Pan-Seared Skillet Crusted Dates
description: Sweet dates coated in a savory crust and seared to perfection. This dish is perfect for a quick and easy weeknight dinner.
instructions: In a small bowl, mix together the flour, smoke paprika, hot paprika, creamy mustard, and pungent garlic powder.
Cut the dates in half lengthwise and remove the pits.
Coat each date half in the flour mixture, pressing the mixture onto the dates to help it stick.
Heat the skillet oil in a large oven-safe skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the coated dates to the skillet and sear for 2-3 minutes on each side, or until golden brown.
Transfer the skillet to the preheated oven and bake for 10-12 minutes, or until the dates are cooked through and the crust is crispy.
Remove the skillet from the oven and let it cool for a few minutes.
Serve the pan-seared dates hot, garnished with additional pungent garlic powder if desired.
